exercises.title: Trace ExecutorService submit and get
exercises.scenario: Future.get() blocks until the task finishes.
hints: f1.get() blocks until (3+4) completes → 7
f2.get() blocks until (10*2) completes → 20

exercises.title: Submit Callable and retrieve result
exercises.scenario: Callable returns a value; Future wraps it.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to submit a Callable and print its result.
hints: pool.submit(callable) returns Future<T>
future.get() blocks and returns the result

exercises.title: Basic thread pool — 3 tasks
exercises.scenario: Submit 3 tasks to a fixed pool and wait for all.
exercises.instructions:
Create a 2-thread pool. Submit 3 Runnable tasks each printing 'Task N done'. Shutdown properly.

Expected output (order of tasks may vary):
Task 1 done
Task 2 done
Task 3 done
All tasks finished
hints: pool.shutdown() stops accepting new tasks
awaitTermination waits for all submitted tasks to finish

exercises.title: Callable returning computed value
exercises.scenario: Callable<T> returns T; Future<T>.get() retrieves it.
exercises.instructions:
Submit a Callable that computes the sum of 1 to 100. Print the result via Future.get().

Expected output:
Sum 1-100 = 5050
hints: Callable<Integer> returns Integer
future.get() blocks and returns the sum

exercises.title: Multiple Futures — parallel computation
exercises.scenario: Submit multiple Callables, collect all results.
exercises.instructions:
Submit 4 Callable tasks to a 4-thread pool. Each computes the square of its index (1-4). Collect via Future.get(), sum the squares, print total.

Expected output:
Squares: [1, 4, 9, 16]
Total: 30
hints: Submit all futures first, then collect in order
futures.get(0), get(1)... preserves submission order

exercises.title: Handle ExecutionException from Callable
exercises.scenario: Task exceptions are wrapped in ExecutionException.
exercises.instructions:
Submit a Callable that throws ArithmeticException (divide by zero). Catch ExecutionException and print the cause.

Expected output:
Task failed: / by zero
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// Handle task exception via ExecutionException
hints: ExecutionException wraps the actual exception
e.getCause() is the original ArithmeticException
ArithmeticException.getMessage() = '/ by zero'

exercises.title: invokeAll — wait for all tasks
exercises.scenario: invokeAll submits and waits for all tasks.
exercises.instructions:
Use invokeAll to submit 5 tasks computing factorials (1! through 5!). Collect and print all results.

Expected output:
1! = 1
2! = 2
3! = 6
4! = 24
5! = 120
hints: invokeAll returns List<Future<T>> in submission order
All futures are done when invokeAll returns — get() won't block
Results are in original submission order despite concurrent execution

exercises.title: invokeAny — fastest response wins
exercises.scenario: invokeAny returns the first successful result.
exercises.instructions:
Submit 3 tasks with different delays (800ms, 200ms, 500ms) returning the delay as a string. Use invokeAny — should return '200ms' (fastest).

Expected output:
Fastest: 200ms responded first
hints: invokeAny returns first successful result, cancels others
200ms task finishes first among 800/200/500
Useful for redundant requests to multiple replicas

exercises.title: Future.get with timeout
exercises.scenario: Timeout prevents blocking forever on a slow task.
exercises.instructions:
Submit a slow task (sleeps 2000ms). Use future.get(500, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS). Catch TimeoutException and print message. Then cancel the future.

Expected output:
Task timed out after 500ms
Cancelled: true
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// Future.get with timeout and cancellation

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Task timed out after 500ms
Cancelled: true

hints: get(500, MILLISECONDS) throws TimeoutException after 500ms if not done
future.cancel(true) interrupts the running thread
shutdownNow() after cancel to clean up the pool

exercises.title: ScheduledExecutorService — delayed and periodic
exercises.scenario: Schedule tasks with delays and fixed rates.
exercises.instructions:
Create a ScheduledExecutorService. Schedule: (1) a one-time task after 200ms printing 'Delayed', (2) a periodic task every 300ms printing 'Tick N'. Run for 1 second, then shutdown.

Expected output:
Delayed
Tick 1
Tick 2
Tick 3
Done
hints: schedule() for one-time delayed task
scheduleAtFixedRate(task, initialDelay, period, unit) for repeating
AtomicInteger for thread-safe counter increment

exercises.title: Parallel stock price fetcher
exercises.scenario: Fan-out pattern: submit N tasks, collect all results.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate fetching prices for 6 stocks in parallel using a 3-thread pool. Each fetch takes 100-300ms. Collect all results and print sorted by price descending.

Stocks: TCS(3890), RELIANCE(2450), INFY(1560), HDFC(1678), WIPRO(412), ZOMATO(184)

Expected output (sorted desc by price):
TCS: ₹3890
RELIANCE: ₹2450
...

test_cases.expected_stdout:
TCS: ₹3890
RELIANCE: ₹2450
HDFC: ₹1678
INFY: ₹1560
WIPRO: ₹412
ZOMATO: ₹184

hints: invokeAll returns futures in submission order
Stream over futures: f -> f.get() wrapped in try-catch
Sort descending: Integer.compare(b.price(), a.price())

exercises.title: Pool size and task ordering
hints: Single-thread executor: tasks run sequentially in submission order
Only one thread — no concurrency — FIFO queue
This is NOT the case with fixedThreadPool(2+) — order could vary

exercises.title: Order processing pipeline — parallel validation
exercises.scenario: Validate an order against multiple services concurrently.
exercises.instructions:
Process an order through 3 parallel validation checks:
1. Stock availability (150ms)
2. Payment authorization (200ms)
3. Address validation (100ms)

All must pass. If any fails (throw exception), report failure.

Expected output:
Stock: OK
Payment: OK
Address: OK
Order accepted!
(or 'Order rejected: [reason]' for failures)

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Stock: OK
Payment: OK
Address: OK
Order accepted!

hints: invokeAll(3 tasks) on 3-thread pool = all run in parallel
Total time ~200ms (longest check) not 450ms sequential
Each Future.get() can throw ExecutionException on failure

exercises.title: Batch processing with thread pool
exercises.instructions:
Process a list of 20 integer values (squaring each) using a 4-thread pool. Batch into groups of 5, submit each batch as one task. Collect all results.

Expected output:
Batch 1: [1, 4, 9, 16, 25]
Batch 2: [36, 49, 64, 81, 100]
Batch 3: [121, 144, 169, 196, 225]
Batch 4: [256, 289, 324, 361, 400]
Total processed: 20
hints: Partition list into sublists of size 5
Submit each sublist as a Callable that maps elements
Futures in submission order — results in correct order

exercises.title: Complete async payment processing system
exercises.scenario: Production-style payment processing with thread pool, timeouts, and fallback.
exercises.instructions:
Process 5 payment requests concurrently using a 3-thread pool. Each payment:
- Validates (50ms)
- Processes (100-300ms, random)
- May fail (20% chance)

For each: print SUCCESS with amount, or FAILED with reason.
For timed-out payments (>400ms): TIMEOUT and cancel.
Print summary: X succeeded, Y failed, Z timed out.

Expected output (values vary):
PAY-001: SUCCESS ₹1500
PAY-002: FAILED card declined
PAY-003: SUCCESS ₹2200
...
Summary: 3 succeeded, 1 failed, 1 timed out
hints: get(400, MILLISECONDS) with TimeoutException for slow payments
ExecutionException for declined cards
Track counts with succeeded/failed/timedOut counters
